members.text: OmegaFlow turns scripted terminal and browser workflows into narrated, rebuildable videos. Start by adding the package to your project's Python environment. When installation finishes, you can run omegaflow from the command line.

Next, from your repository root, run bootstrap to set up the recording workspace. The command creates the project settings, recording defaults, and workspace layout. The generated quickstart is ready to run, so you can try OmegaFlow immediately.
Then, build the sample recording. OmegaFlow runs the scripted workflow and creates a ready-to-watch player. When the build finishes, the follow-up command shows how to watch the video.

OmegaFlow controls the browser during the recording. To demonstrate this, we'll open the player for the video we just built, then play the video. A single OmegaFlow video can move between terminal and browser beats, while one narration take keeps every action synchronized with the voiceover. To learn more, start the tutorial or read the docs.
